intro:
There is not much information on Niebuhria triphylla, however, I have found some information on 'Nepeta' or 'Nepeta cataria', which seems to be a similar plant. 

Niebuhria triphylla, which may be a different name for Nepeta, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to promote digestion, alleviate headaches, and reduce anxiety. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils such as nepetalactone, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Nepeta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical applications for skin and muscle relief.
